name: Use of Inner Class Containing Sensitive Data
description: An inner class contains sensitive data, but the Java compiler transforms inner classes into peer classes with package-level access. Private fields accessed by the inner class are converted to protected fields in bytecode, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive data from any class in the same package.
detection_pattern: STRUCTURAL(inner_class) containing STORAGE(sensitive_data) where the compiler transforms access modifiers. Look for STORAGE(private_field) in an inner class context that becomes accessible at package scope, exposing data to EXTERNAL(same_package_class) without AUTH(access_control).
